title: Visual Mapping Indicators in the Data Table
story: As a data migration specialist, I want to see which fields have been mapped directly in the main data table, so that I can quickly identify mapping progress without opening each field's detail panel.
description:
When a user maps a field in the side panel, two updates should be reflected in the main data table:

1. **Hyperlink text update**: The "Click to Mapping" label is replaced with `[SourceObject].[FieldName]` (e.g., "Deal.Other") showing the mapped source.
2. **Status update**: The field's migration status is set to "In Progress".

For fields mapped to multiple source objects, all mapped field names are displayed in the data table. When a mapping is deleted, the label reverts to "Click to Mapping" and the status updates accordingly.

The label is now dynamically derived from a MySQL query rather than updating a label column on each mapping change, ensuring consistency.
acceptance_criteria: Mapped fields display the source object and field name (e.g., 'Deal.Other') in the data table
Unmapped fields show 'Click to Mapping' as the default label
Migration status is set to 'In Progress' when a field is mapped
Multiple mapped sources are all displayed in the data table
Deleting a mapping reverts the label and status
Labels are dynamically derived from the mapping data, not stored as static text
ux_changes: Data table column shows dynamic mapping labels (SourceObject.FieldName) instead of static 'Click to Mapping' text. Status column auto-updates to 'In Progress' on mapping.
middleware_changes: Label generation moved to a MySQL query that dynamically constructs the display text from mapping relationships rather than updating a label column on each change.
api_changes: Updated data table API to include dynamically generated mapping labels derived from the mapping relationships.
implementation_notes: Initially the labels were updated by directly modifying a label column on each mapping change. This was refactored to use a MySQL query that dynamically constructs the label from the mapping data, which is more reliable and handles multi-source scenarios correctly. The 1:n mapping support was already in the data model.
comment_summary: Discussion centered on handling multi-source mappings — whether to show all mapped fields or limit to 1:1. Decided to display all mapped sources. Developer implemented dynamic label generation from MySQL query instead of static column updates. Label reversion on delete was added as a follow-up.
